vocab.word: variety is the spice of life
vocab.meaning: thay đổi là hương vị của cuộc sống (sự đa dạng làm cuộc sống thú vị hơn)
vocab.example: I enjoy views in both my country and abroad because variety is the spice of life, and I love seeing different landscapes.

vocab.word: beauty is in the eye of the beholder
vocab.ipa: /ˈbjuːti ɪz ɪn ði aɪ əv ðə bɪˈhəʊldə(r)/
vocab.meaning: vẻ đẹp nằm trong mắt kẻ si tình (tùy quan điểm mỗi người)
vocab.example: Whether a view in my country is better than one abroad is subjective, as beauty is truly in the eye of the beholder.

vocab.word: home is where the heart is
vocab.meaning: nhà là nơi trái tim thuộc về (nơi thân thuộc nhất)
vocab.example: No matter how grand the mountains are in Europe, I still prefer our local hills because home is where the heart is.

vocab.word: the grass is always greener on the other side
vocab.ipa: /ðə ɡrɑːs ɪz ˈɔːlweɪz ˈɡriːnə(r) ɒn ði ˈʌðə(r) saɪd/
vocab.meaning: đứng núi này trông núi nọ
vocab.example: We often think foreign views are superior, but that's just a case of the grass being greener on the other side.
I prefer traveling to other countries to see their unique views as it really helps to broaden my horizons.
I only get to see snow-capped mountains once in a blue moon when I travel abroad, which makes the view more special.
Before going overseas, I want to explore every nook and cranny of my own country to see all its hidden natural beauty.

vocab.meaning: thử mọi cách/tìm kiếm khắp nơi
vocab.example: I've left no stone unturned in my search for the best coastal views along our country's long shoreline.
I usually steer clear of famous tourist viewpoints abroad because they are often too crowded for my liking.

vocab.meaning: mỗi người một ý/tùy sở thích mỗi người
vocab.example: Some people find city views abroad more exciting, but I prefer the rural views here; each to their own, I guess.
